title: Types of Quantitative Research Design - Comprehensive Quiz

questions.question: When little is known about the research problem, which research design is most appropriate to use?
Descriptive research is exploratory in nature and answers questions such as:
options: Who, what, where, when, and how much

questions.question: Descriptive research is useful when the research aim is to identify characteristics, frequencies, trends, and:
Which of the following is an example of a descriptive research question?
options: Is there a relationship between technology use and mental health?
What are the most popular online news sources among under-18s?
Does social media use affect adolescent self-esteem?
What is the effect of giving daily quizzes on student performance?

questions.question: Correlational research seeks to determine if two variables are related or if there is:
Correlational research can take several forms, including direct observation and fieldwork, use of archival data, and:
Correlational research only observes naturally occurring variables without:
The main goal of correlational research is to determine if a variable increases or decreases as:
Correlational research does NOT seek which type of relationship?
Correlational research has two major purposes: (a) to clarify the relationship between variables and (b) to:
options: Predict the magnitude of the association

questions.question: Which research design looks at existing conditions and works backwards to identify relationships or causal factors?
The term 'Ex Post Facto' means:
Ex post facto research is also known as:
In ex post facto research, the variables of interest have already occurred and their effects are being studied:
Which research design suggests a non-manipulative approach where variables have already occurred?
The prefix 'quasi' means:
Quasi-experimental research resembles experimental research but is NOT:
Quasi-experimental research aims to measure the causal relationship between variables. The effect measured is considered to have occurred:
options: During the conduct of the current study

questions.question: In quasi-experimental research, the groups are known to be already established before the study, such as age, educational background, and:
Quasi-experimental methods do NOT include which key feature of true experiments?
Which of the following is an example of quasi-experimental research?
options: Randomly assigning plants to sunlight and dark conditions
Comparing non-smokers to smokers (traits that cannot be randomly assigned)
A/B testing two versions of a software interface
Testing different advertising messages on random participants

questions.question: Experimental research is also known as:
Experimental research is based on the scientific method called experiment, which involves gathering data under a:
In experimental research, random assignment of subjects into treatment and control groups increases the:
Experimental research attempts to affect a certain variable by directly manipulating the:
Which of the following is an example of experimental research?
options: Studying a specific classroom of students to determine learning outcomes
A researcher randomly assigns half the plant samples to sunlight and half to a dark box
Comparing the impact of social media on adolescent self-esteem after the fact
Determining if there is a relationship between life expectancy and paint color

questions.question: A Software as a Service (SaaS) company conducting A/B tests to compare two versions of its software interface is an example of:
The treatment group refers to the group subjected to treatment or intervention, while the group not subjected is called the:
How many mistakes to avoid while designing research are listed in the PDF?
According to the 'Mistakes to Avoid,' researchers often miss out on checking if their hypothesis is logical to be tested. This refers to:
Without a comprehensive research literature review, it is difficult to identify and fill knowledge and information gaps. This refers to:
Incorrect statistical analysis could affect the quality of any quantitative research. This refers to:
The research problem statement must be clear and set the framework for the development of research questions. This refers to avoiding:
You should anticipate and incorporate limitations into your conclusion as well as the basic research design. This refers to:
Your research design must include ways to minimize any risk for your participants. This refers to:
If you cannot manage ethical norms along with your research study, your research objectives and validity could be questioned. This refers to:
The four types of quantitative research designs mentioned that are NOT experimental include: Descriptive, Correlational, _____, and Quasi-Experimental.
The two types of quantitative research designs that are identifiable for the existence of treatment or intervention are Quasi-Experimental and _____.
Which research design uses random sampling and random assignment to create a control group and an experimental group?
Which research design is appropriate when the main objective is just to observe and report a phenomenon as it is happening?
